summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter DownBound code by moving much of the 0x80097570-0x80097E40 range out of ft_08A4 assembly into a new ftCo_DownBound.c/h pair, while keeping unresolved or nonmatching portions guarded by MUST_MATCH inline assembly. The PR also renamed the leftover assembly slice from ft_4D3A.s to ftCo_DownBound.s, wired the new C object into obj_files.mk, relocated the DownBound sound ID table out of ftdynamics.c, and tightened related fighter header prototypes and types. The score report showed several fully matched functions, including ftCo_80097570, ftCo_80097630, and the DownBound Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, with larger transition/effect helpers still partial.
what_changed: Added a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound.c containing 802 lines of C and guarded inline assembly for the DownBound code range.

Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound.h with public prototypes for ftCo_80097570, ftCo_80097630, ftCo_800976A4, ftCo_800978D4, ftCo_8009794C, ftCo_80097AF4, ft_80097D40, ftCo_80097D88, and the ftCo_DownBound Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks.
Removed most of the corresponding DownBound assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and renamed asm/melee/ft/ft_4D3A.s to asm/melee/ft/ftCo_DownBound.s for the remaining assembly slice.
Updated obj_files.mk to build the new ftCo_DownBound.c object and the renamed ftCo_DownBound.s object instead of the old ft_4D3A.s object alone.

Introduced ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds as a local DownBound symbol with values { 9, 10, 11, 12 } and removed the previous ftDynamics_803C5700 table from ftdynamics.c; nearby assembly references were updated to use ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds.
Added includes for ftCo_DownBound.h in dependent common fighter and Ness files that reference DownBound helpers.
Changed ftCommonData fields x1F0 and x1F4 from UNK_T to float, matching their use as floating thresholds in DownBound sound/effect selection.
Refined many ft_081B.h prototypes from generic HSD_GObj or UNK_RET forms to Fighter_GObj/ftCo_GObj and bool-returning signatures, including ft_80084C74.

Changed tools/report_score.py percent calculation to clamp negative match percentages at 0 with max(0, ...), matching the PR report where over-budget functions display 0.00% rather than negative percentages.
smart_moves: Kept the PR explicitly partial: functions that were not cleanly matched remained under MUST_MATCH asm blocks while matched or near-matched logic was expressed in C, allowing progress without pretending the whole file was solved.
Moved the sound ID array from the generic ftdynamics area to a DownBound-named symbol, which made ownership clearer and let both the new C and remaining assembly refer to a semantically named table.
Preserved m2c stack knowledge in ftCo_DownBound.h under M2CTX with a stack struct for ftCo_800976A4, giving future work a documented stack layout without polluting normal builds.
Used typed prototypes and Fighter_GObj/ftCo_GObj names in headers to support decompilation of call sites and reduce reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Introduced small local helpers such as ftCo_80097630 for selecting SFX from threshold buckets, reducing duplicated threshold logic in the decompiled DownBound flow.
Documented unresolved matching issues directly in the C file with TODOs for unused stack padding and a suspected common inline/library function, making future cleanup targets searchable.
decomp_lessons: For partially matched fighter states, split the code into a state-specific ftCo_* C file and leave only truly unresolved ranges in assembly; update obj_files.mk in the same change so build ordering and symbol ownership stay coherent.

When an anonymous data symbol is only used by one state or mechanic, rename and relocate it to that mechanic's file if the diff evidence supports it; here ftDynamics_803C5700 became ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds.
Do not leave cross-file prototypes in broad headers after a state-specific header is introduced; move DownBound declarations from ft_08A4.h/ft_0877.h-style generic locations toward ftCo_DownBound.h.

Typing common-data fields as float can be justified by repeated fcmpo/lfs threshold use; ftCommonData.x1F0 and x1F4 were changed from unknowns to float because DownBound compares impact distance against them.
If matching requires exact stack artifacts, isolate them with MUST_MATCH guards or M2CTX-only structs rather than forcing misleading runtime code.
Score tooling should handle over-budget functions gracefully; clamping percent at 0 avoids confusing negative scores for very nonmatching functions.
naming_conventions: Use the state/mechanic prefix ftCo_DownBound for files, headers, state callbacks, and owned data such as ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds.
Common fighter state callbacks follow the suffix pattern _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll: ftCo_DownBound_Anim, ftCo_DownBound_IASA, ftCo_DownBound_Phys, ftCo_DownBound_Coll.
Keep address-based placeholder function names when semantic names are not known, e.g. ftCo_800976A4, ftCo_800978D4, ftCo_8009794C, ftCo_80097AF4.
Prefer typed fighter object aliases in prototypes, such as Fighter_GObj* and ftCo_GObj*, over broad HSD_GObj* when call context is known.
Use M2CTX stack struct names that include the target function, e.g. _m2c_stack_ftCo_800976A4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) blocks to retain original assembly for functions that were still hard to match while allowing C fallbacks during WIP builds.
Matched small callbacks directly: ftCo_DownBound_IASA is an empty function, while Anim/Phys/Coll delegate to existing helpers, contributing 100% scores for those functions.
Used explicit unused stack padding under MUST_MATCH in several C alternatives to reproduce stack layout where the compiler otherwise would not allocate the same space.
Preserved low-level behavior around HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub, atan2f, efAsync_Spawn, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ft_8009F834, Camera_80030E44, and ft_80088148 in the C reconstruction.

The PR report showed mixed matching quality: ftCo_80097570, ftCo_80097630, ftCo_DownBound_Anim, ftCo_DownBound_Coll, ftCo_DownBound_Phys, and ftCo_DownBound_IASA were 100%, while ftCo_800976A4 was high but not perfect and ft_80097D40/ftCo_80097D88 were still 0%.
Renamed the leftover assembly file to match the newly identified subsystem, making remaining assembly easier to locate by searching ftCo_DownBound instead of ft_4D3A.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_800976A4, ftCo_800978D4, ftCo_8009794C, ftCo_80097AF4, ft_80097D40, or ftCo_80097D88.
Search for TODOs about the common inlineA0 helper or similar efAsync_Spawn wrapper to see whether it became a shared header/helper later.
Search for subsequent changes to ftCommonData.x1F0/x1F4 names to recover semantic field names for DownBound impact thresholds.
Search for later cleanup of MUST_MATCH unused stack padding in ftCo_DownBound.c.
Check whether ftCo_DownBound_SfxIds remained in this file or was renamed again after more semantic recovery.
